title: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1

'''''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1''' is the first issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] miniseries ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]''. It was written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]], illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], and first published on [[August 15]], [[1995]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

The issue is set six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the events of [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]], and it features the story "'''Edge of the Whirlwind'''," in which [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] challenges the fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] to a [[Duel on Kuar|duel]].

==Publisher's summary==
''This issue kicks off the thrill-a-minute follow-up to the best-selling ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' saga. Picking up six months after our story left off, Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma are implementing their insidious plan for revitalizing the Golden Age of the Sith. While insinuating his darkside teachings to Jedi students on the planet Ossus, it becomes clear that Kun is only concerned with securing a powerful Sith Holocron from its sealed repository in the great library of Ossus. Ulic has devoted himself to developing the darkside military strategy -- the key to which may just be winning the loyalty of the ferocious warrior race led by a masked mysteryman named Mandalore''.

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Edge of the Whirlwind
|text=It has been six months since the former
enemies Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma have
joined forces, forced into a common goal by
hypnotic visions of a reborn Golden Age of
the Sith.

Through his dabbling in forbidden teachings,
Exar Kun has fallen completely under the spell of
the ancient Sith ways, and he knows he must
gain additional disciples to fan the flames of his
planned victory.

Meanwhile, in the iron-walled city of Cinnagar in the
Empress Teta system, Ulic Qel-Droma has stumbled down
the dark path — driven by a distorted need to avenge the
murder of his beloved Master Arca, poisoned by dark Sith
toxins, and seduced and manipulated by his wily lover
Aleema. He has allied himself with Exar Kun to bring about
a new Golden Age, following the dream-images that fog his
mind. His mission is to gather an army awesome enough to
take on even the mighty galactic Republic.

But as their plans proceed, far from the watchful eyes of the
loyal Jedi Knights, other vicious crusaders turn their attentions to
what may seem to be easy pickings while Ulic is preoccupied…}}

==Plot summary==
===Mandalorian crusaders===
The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], led by [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], wage war on the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]]. Mandalorian warriors riding [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] attack an orbital Tetan [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] smelter. They disable it with an [[atomic compression bomb]], causing it to crash into a city on a planet's surface.

Regarding the Mandalorian invasion as an obstacle to [[Great Sith War/Legends|his larger plan]], the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] parleys with Mandalore, who challenges Ulic to a duel. Ulic accepts Mandalore's challenge on the terms that if he wins, he shall receive control of the Mandalorian warriors.

===Subversion on Ossus===
Meanwhile, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] visits [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus']] [[Gardens of T'alla/Legends|Gardens of T'alla]], meeting with several Jedi apprentices. He claims to have unearthed forgotten [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] secrets and powers and offers to share them with other Jedi. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Oss Wilum]] is concerned that Kun is asking them to leave their [[Jedi Master/Legends|masters]] and accept him as their teacher while [[Crado]] reminds Kun that Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] has not yet declared Kun a master yet.

Kun claims the Jedi Masters are suspicious of his teachings and criticizes the late Master [[Arca Jeth]] for letting [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] escape his grasp. An apprentice named [[Zona Luka]] agrees with Kun but [[Cay Qel-Droma]] counters that Master Arca sacrificed is own life to save other Jedi. When Wilum mentions the fallen Jedi spirit of [[Freedon Nadd]], Kun claims that he hunted down and vanquished Nadd's spirit without the help of Arca. As proof, Kun displays his Jedi amulet and invites the other Jedi to join him in learning ancient Jedi secrets.

===Duel on Kuar===
On [[Kuar/Legends|Kuar]], Ulic faces Mandalore the Indomitable in a duel over two fallen towers suspended by chain. Ulic wields his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] while Mandalore rides a Basilisk war droid. Despite being outgunned by Mandalore, Ulic uses his Force powers to separate Mandalore from his war mount.

During the second round, Mandalore wields a [[Mythosaur axe/Legends|Mythosaur axe]]. He manages to gain an advantage over Ulic but Ulic uses his [[The Force/Legends|Force powers]] to leap onto a nearby war droid. Armed with the war droid, Ulic demands that Mandalore surrender. Mandalore is defiant but Ulic attacks Mandalore with the war droid, causing the Mandalorian leader to fall off his vantage point.

Accepting defeat, Mandalore cedes his warriors to Ulic and asks him to kill him. However, Ulic reassures Mandalore that he is not interested in his death but has a "much better fate" in mind.

===Enemy within the ranks===
Meanwhile, [[Nomi Sunrider]] continues her training in Jedi [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] under the tutelage of Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] in the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] on Ossus. Nomi's daughter [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]] is also present. Master Odan-Urr tells her that the ancient Sith Lords built meditation chambers and that the best way to combat Sith battle meditation is to use her Jedi abilities to take power away from her opponent; effectively stripping them of the Force.

Odan-Urr also has a [[Dark Holocron|Sith holocron]] containing the forgotten histories and lore of the Sith dating back a [[100,000 BBY/Legends|hundred thousand years]]. Nomi and Vima, whom Odan-Urr predicts will be a Jedi Master with many apprentices, leave the Jedi Master's chambers. Shortly later, the Sith holocron starts glowing and Exar Kun uses the Force to summon it.

Odan-Urr tries to drive Kun away with the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] but the ancient Jedi Master is no match for the Dark Lord, who kills him. After Odan-Urr becomes one with the Force, his Jedi followers enter the chamber. Kun claims that Odan-Urr died peacefully but gifted the holocron to him.

===Raid on Foerost===
A gigantic ship called ''[[Golthar's Sky]]'' arrives at the [[Foerost Shipyards]] seeking repairs. However, the giant ship is actually a Force illusion conjured up by Aleema Keto to hide a combined Krath and Mandalorian attack force. The Foerost Shipyards are overwhelmed and boarded by Mandalorian warriors, Tetan soldiers, and Basilisk war droids while [[CX-133 Chaos fighter]]s take out the defensive cannons.

Ulic forces the surviving shipyard workers to hand him the operations codes for the shipyard's 300 warships. Shortly later, Ulic is contacted via [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] by his master Dark Lord Exar Kun, who informs him that he has recruited 20 Jedi Knights. Ulic boasts that he commands two armies and 300 of the newest Republic warships. While Kun believes that they should gather more ships while he trains his Jedi, Ulic proposes attacking the galactic capital [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] since the Republic's military is stretched defending its shipards and outposts.

Kun disagrees and says that they should build up their forces before attacking together. Mandalore the Indomitable backs up Ulic and reassures Kun that his clans are the best fighters in the galaxy. Kun allows Ulic to proceed with his plan but warns that if he fails, he will go on without him. Ulic is determined to prove that the Republic is weak and vulnerable.

[[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] reinforcements led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Vanicus]] arrive at the Foerost Shipyards. While rescuing survivors and checking the station's automated security systems, Vanicus and his troops discover a holo-recording of Ulic and Mandalore discussing a surprise attack against [[Kemplex Nine]]. Vanicus recognizes Ulic and laments that this is a dark day for the Republic.

==Continuity==
In ''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1'', [[Zona Luka]] says that [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] died on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], and [[Oss Wilum]] explains that it happened by an avatar of [[Freedon Nadd]]. The previously published [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]] establishes that he died in the [[Conclave at Deneba]] by a [[Krath war droid/Legends|Krath war droid]].<ref name="Dark Lords of the Sith 3">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]]</ref>
